title: Good: DAO route assembly uses bounded O(1) loop detection
description: Good decision: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/routing.c uses a bounded open-addressed visited_set for DAO path assembly. Lines 36-42 define VISITED_BUCKETS with power-of-two and max-hop static asserts, lines 70-93 implement check-and-add with table-full treated as a loop, and lines 448-453 document why assemble_path() is O(E * H) instead of drifting toward O(E * H^2). This is worth preserving if someone suggests simplifying back to nested scans: the current design keeps route rebuild cost bounded without dynamic allocation and has clear failure behavior for loops or overlong chains.
close_reason: Preserved: verified bounded visited_set loop detection and O(E * H) DAO assembly comments remain after RPL fixes; no code change required for this positive review decision.
